evals.prompt: Write a README for this project.
evals.context: Python FastAPI project with docker-compose.yml, no existing README.
assertions: Output file is README.md written to the repo root
Contains a one-line description of what the project does
Contains a Quick Start section with at least one runnable command
Uses actual commands from the codebase (not generic 'pip install myapp')
Does not mention Neo4j, Kubernetes, or other infrastructure not present in the project
Does not contain empty sections

evals.prompt: Update the README for this project.
evals.context: Scala Maven library with a README that has API usage examples but no build or test instructions.
assertions: Existing API examples are preserved unchanged
A Build section is added with the correct mvn command
A Testing section is added with the correct mvn test command
The file is edited (not fully rewritten)
No sections are duplicated

evals.prompt: Generate a README for this project.
evals.context: Project whose README says 'Neo4j' but the code uses SQLite (aiosqlite). Multiple sections contradict the code.
assertions: Skill does NOT write a new README before asking the user
Skill surfaces the specific contradictions (Neo4j vs SQLite) as questions
Questions are batched into a single message (not one at a time)
No more than 3 questions are asked

evals.context: Full-stack project with Python backend, React/Vite frontend, and Docker Compose.
assertions: Output file is ARCHITECTURE.md written to the repo root
Contains a Mermaid diagram with both backend and frontend as distinct nodes
Describes both stacks — not just one
Contains a data flow section showing a request moving through the system
Does not fabricate components not present in the code

evals.prompt: Write an onboarding guide for this project.
evals.context: Python + TypeScript project with a start.sh launcher, .github/workflows/ci.yml, and no existing ONBOARDING.md.
assertions: Output file is ONBOARDING.md
Prerequisites section lists both Python and Node.js with version numbers sourced from CI config
Setup section references start.sh rather than duplicating its steps manually
Running tests section includes single-test commands for both stacks
Writing a new test section describes the actual fixture pattern found in the test files
Common issues section contains at least one real failure mode (not invented)

evals.context: Python app with docker-compose.yml (dev and prod profiles), no Kubernetes, no CI deploy job, no image registry configured.
assertions: Output file is DEPLOYMENT.md
Documents both the dev and prod compose profiles with the correct commands
Environment variables table is present with Required/Default columns
Health check section documents the /health endpoint
Rollback section is present and provides the git-based fallback pattern (not left empty)
Does not mention Kubernetes, Helm, or other infrastructure not present

evals.prompt: Generate API documentation for this project.
evals.context: Python FastAPI project with no JSDoc or docstrings on route handlers. Routes are in routes.py with Pydantic models in models.py.
assertions: Output file is docs/api.md
Contains an Authentication section describing the auth mechanism
Each endpoint has Method, Path, and Description
At least one endpoint includes a sample response JSON
Does not document internal helpers or private functions
Does not fabricate endpoints not present in the routes file

evals.prompt: Update the docs for my new feature.
evals.context: Feature branch added: (1) POST /users/reset-password route in routes.py, (2) EMAIL_SERVICE_URL env var in config.py. Existing docs/api.md and DEPLOYMENT.md are present. README.md exists but no user-facing behavior changed.
assertions: Skill runs git diff main...HEAD --name-only before reading any source files
Only docs/api.md and DEPLOYMENT.md are edited — README.md is not touched
POST /users/reset-password is added to docs/api.md
EMAIL_SERVICE_URL is added to the env vars table in DEPLOYMENT.md
No existing content in either doc is removed or rewritten
Report names exactly which sections were updated

evals.prompt: Generate a README and put it in the docs/ folder.
evals.context: Python FastAPI project, no existing docs/. User explicitly said 'put it in docs/'.
assertions: Output file is docs/README.md — not README.md at the repo root
docs/ directory is created if it does not exist
Content follows the same README quality rules as the readme subcommand
No file is written to the repo root

evals.prompt: Write an architecture doc and an API reference for our GitHub wiki.
evals.context: Node.js/Express project with a REST API. User said 'for our GitHub wiki'.
assertions: Output filenames are capitalised and flat: Architecture.md and API-Reference.md (not docs/api.md)
Internal cross-references use [[Page Name]] syntax, not markdown links
No YAML front matter in either file
Mermaid diagram is still present in Architecture.md
Content sections are otherwise identical to what the arch and api subcommands would produce

evals.prompt: Update the docs for my new feature.
evals.context: Feature branch changed routes.py (new endpoint). No docs/api.md exists yet.
assertions: Skill does not create docs/api.md as part of the update pass
Skill tells the user docs/api.md is missing and suggests running /code-to-docs api first
No other files are created or edited

evals.context: Python FastAPI project using Redis as the primary data store (no relational DB), SQLite for audit logs, and Celery for background jobs. No existing docs/adr/ directory.
assertions: Output directory is docs/adr/ with numbered files (e.g. 001-*.md, 002-*.md)
Each file follows the format: Status / Context / Decision / Consequences
Redis-as-primary-store ADR includes a clarifying question before writing (surprising choice)
PostgreSQL or other obvious-default choices are NOT questioned
Questions are batched in a single message, at most 5 total
Context section uses the placeholder pattern when rationale cannot be inferred
Does not produce more than 8 ADRs total
Does not document naming conventions or dependency versions as ADRs

evals.context: Project with docs/adr/001-use-mongodb.md (status: accepted) but the codebase now uses PostgreSQL via SQLAlchemy. No other ADRs exist.
assertions: Skill reads existing ADRs before writing anything
Creates a new ADR (e.g. 002-migrate-to-postgresql.md) that supersedes ADR-001
Updates the status field of ADR-001 to 'superseded by ADR-002'
Does not delete or fully rewrite ADR-001

evals.prompt: Document the test strategy for this project.
evals.context: Python FastAPI project with tests/unit/ and tests/integration/, conftest.py with a client fixture and a db fixture, testcontainers used in integration tests, pytest-cov configured with 80% threshold, and a GitHub Actions workflow that runs unit and integration tests as separate jobs.
assertions: Output file is TESTING.md at the repo root
Documents both unit and integration layers with separate subsections
Mocking boundary is explicitly stated: unit tests mock HTTP and DB; integration tests use real testcontainers DB
Coverage threshold (80%) is documented with the command to run it
CI section documents the two separate GitHub Actions jobs
Writing a test section shows the actual conftest.py fixture pattern
Does not duplicate the single-test invocation commands already in ONBOARDING.md
Does not invent test layers or patterns not present in the code

evals.prompt: Document the architecture of this project.
evals.context: Large codebase with 10+ architectural decisions that can't be inferred from code alone.
assertions: Skill asks at most 3 clarifying questions before writing
Questions are batched into a single message
Remaining gaps are noted inline in the document rather than asked as additional questions
